scenario_title: Add CNAME and A at the same owner in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test targets the protocol gap around simultaneous creation of a CNAME and another RR type at the same owner in one dynamic update. Because the UPDATE contains no prerequisites and only two conflicting add operations, a server must choose between rejecting the message, applying operations in an internal order, or otherwise repairing exclusivity. The two follow-up queries for CNAME and A make the surviving RRset, if any, visible and distinguishable from a clean rejection that leaves the name absent.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE or preserves one RRset according to its internal processing order, leaving a single surviving RRset at alias.campus.edu. or possibly rejecting with YXRRSET; post-state is observable via follow-up queries.
group_1.post_state: Either CNAME-only or A-only at alias.campus.edu., depending on implementation behavior.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE to enforce CNAME exclusivity, typically with YXRRSET, leaving alias.campus.edu. absent.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines the wire format for multiple add_rr operations in one UPDATE but does not specify how the server must resolve intra-message creation of mutually exclusive RRsets at the same owner name. RFC 2181 §10.1 says a node with a CNAME should have no other data, but it does not define update-order semantics or whether the server should reject the whole message versus preserve one RRset implicitly. This leaves the reply code and resulting post-state underspecified.
